text: Mars once thrived with a thick atmosphere... so what happened? Unlike Earth, Mars has a weak magnetic shield. This made it vulnerable to solar winds. Solar winds stripped away essential particles, leading to a thinning atmosphere over millions of years. Adding to the problem, low gravity makes it hard for Mars to hold onto lighter gases. With these forces combined, Mars lost its thick atmosphere, leaving a barren landscape. Want to dive deeper into space mysteries? Like and follow for more!
